feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
test_steps: Configure the interface for 100 Kbps normal-mode operation.
Apply I2C activity on SCL and SDA.
Observe operation at the configured speed.
expected_results: The interface operates in 100 Kbps normal mode.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation, where implemented.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Normal: 100Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applicable where correct hardware is included.
test_steps: Configure the interface for 400 Kbps fast-mode operation.
Apply I2C activity on SCL and SDA.
Observe operation at the configured speed.
expected_results: The interface operates in 400 Kbps fast mode.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation, where correct hardware is included.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Fast: 400Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core provides a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
test_steps: Apply WISHBONE bus transactions using wb_clk_i and wb_rst_i.
Observe bus handshaking on wb_stb_i, wb_cyc_i, wb_ack_o, and related signals.
Check that the interface behaves as a WISHBONE Classic bus.
expected_results: The interface responds as a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
The listed WISHBONE signals are exercised in the transaction.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core shall implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.requirement_text: The core features a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.

feature_list.test_description: Verify each WISHBONE access completes in two clock cycles.
test_steps: Initiate a WISHBONE access.
Count wb_clk_i cycles until wb_ack_o responds.
Repeat for a representative access.
expected_results: Each access completes in 2 clock cycles.
wb_ack_o indicates completion on the expected cycle.
feature_list.requirement_description: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset can be configured as active high.
feature_list.test_constraints: Reset level configured to active high (1'b1).
test_steps: Configure ARST_LVL for active-high reset.
Assert arst_i high.
Observe reset response.
expected_results: The core enters reset when arst_i is high.
Reset behavior matches the configured active-high level.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset can be configured as active low.
feature_list.test_constraints: Reset level configured to active low (1'b0).
test_steps: Configure ARST_LVL for active-low reset.
Assert arst_i low.
Observe reset response.
expected_results: The core enters reset when arst_i is low.
Reset behavior matches the configured active-low level.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify SCL pad input, output, and output-enable signals are exposed for external open-drain bus connection.
test_steps: Check availability of scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe.
Drive or observe the SCL pad-related signals in a bus connection scenario.
Confirm the signals are usable for external connection.
expected_results: scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe are present.
The SCL pad interface supports external open-drain connection.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable

feature_list.test_description: Verify SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals are exposed for external open-drain bus connection.
test_steps: Check availability of sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe.
Drive or observe the SDA pad-related signals in a bus connection scenario.
Confirm the signals are usable for external connection.
expected_results: sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe are present.
The SDA pad interface supports external open-drain connection.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable
